{
 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "72aa7e2b-1248-4899-b79d-f5ffbd9344d1",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# Optical Interference and Multilayer Photonics Simulation\n",
    "\n",
    "Author: Juan David Fernández Dussán \n",
    "\n",
    "Degree: Physics (3rd year) \n",
    "\n",
    "Language: Python\n",
    "\n",
    "---\n",
    "\n",
    "## Project Overview\n",
    "\n",
    "This project develops a progressive numerical study of optical interference phenomena, starting from basic two-wave interference and culminating in the full Transfer Matrix Method (TMM) for arbitrary multilayer dielectric systems.\n",
    "\n",
    "The objective is to connect analytical wave optics with computational modeling, validating each step trough physical consistency checks.\n",
    "\n",
    "---\n",
    "\n",
    "## Project Structure\n",
    "\n",
    "Week 1 - Two-wave interference\n",
    "Week 2 - Fresnel Coefficients and Energy Conservation\n",
    "Week 3 - Thin Film Interference and Fabry-Pérot Resonator\n",
    "Week 4 - Transfer Matrix Method and Multilayer Systems\n",
    "\n",
    "Figures are organized in subfloders by weeks.\n",
    "\n",
    "---\n",
    "\n",
    "## Key Physical Concepts Covered\n",
    "\n",
    "- Monochromatic plane waves\n",
    "- Phase accumulation\n",
    "- Fresnel reflection and transmission\n",
    "- Energy conservation (R + T = 1)\n",
    "- Thin film interference\n",
    "- Fabry-Pérot resonances\n",
    "- Distributed Bragg reflectors\n",
    "- Photonic stop bands\n",
    "- Transfer Matrix formalism\n",
    "\n",
    "---\n",
    "\n",
    "## Numerical Validation\n",
    "\n",
    "The implementation includes two independent physical validation steps:\n",
    "\n",
    "1. Energy conservation verification:\n",
    "\n",
    "R + T = 1\n",
    "\n",
    "Numerical deviation is on the order of $10^{-16}$ (machine presicion)\n",
    "\n",
    "2. Analytical validation of Bragg mirror stop band width:\n",
    "\n",
    "$\\Delta\\lambda = \\dfrac{4\\lambda_0}{\\pi}\\arcsin\\left(\\dfrac{n_H - n_L}{n_H + n_L}\\right)$\n",
    "\n",
    "The numerical and analytical results show strong agreement.\n",
    "\n",
    "---\n",
    "\n",
    "## Tools Used\n",
    "\n",
    "- Phyton\n",
    "- Numpy\n",
    "- Matplotlib\n",
    "\n",
    "---\n",
    "\n",
    "## Scientific References\n",
    "\n",
    "- Hecht, E. - *Optics*\n",
    "- Born & Wolf - *Principle of Optics* (conceptual reference)\n",
    "- Classical thin-film optics formalism"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
